title: Surface backpressure as a first-class signal
description: Monitor and bound buffering so a slow downstream applies measurable backpressure instead of silently growing latency or OOMing.
body: Use when a stream operator can outpace its downstream. Do not let buffers grow unboundedly to hide a slow sink — latency creeps up and the job eventually OOMs with no clear cause. Actions: enable the engine's backpressure mechanism, expose a backpressure/busy metric, set bounded buffers, and alert when sustained backpressure appears. Validation signals include a backpressure metric that rises under a seeded slow sink and bounded buffer sizes under load. Risk boundary: hidden backpressure turns into a sudden outage; if backpressure is sustained, escalate to scale the bottleneck operator rather than enlarging buffers to mask it.
